article_title: Forgejo 15.0 Adds Repository-Scoped Tokens, OIDC Support, and Ephemeral Runners in New LTS Release

content_preview.title: Forgejo 15.0 Adds Repository-Scoped Tokens, OIDC Support, and Ephemeral Runners in New LTS Release
content_preview.summary: Forgejo 15.0 lands as the project's new LTS release with repository-scoped access tokens, reusable workflow expansion, OpenID Connect support, and ephemeral runners.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Forgejo v15.0 shipped on April 16, 2026 as the project's 100th release and its new long-term-support branch, according to the [official Forgejo announcement](https://forgejo.org/2026-04-release-v15-0/), [LWN](https://lwn.net/Articles/1068001/), and the [Forgejo release schedule](https://forgejo.org/docs/latest/admin/release-schedule/). The release keeps pushing the self-hosted Git forge toward more opinionated security and automation defaults, with repository-scoped access tokens, e...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 658 words (within the 400-1200 range). Clear Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, and Analysis sections. Technical claims are presented with appropriate specificity and all inline citations are properly attributed.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources fetched and verified. (1) forgejo.org/2026-04-release-v15-0/ — official release post confirms April 16 date, 100th release milestone, LTS designation, repository-scoped tokens, OIDC support, ephemeral runners, reusable workflow expansion, breaking cookie-name and rootless Docker config changes. (2) forgejo.org/docs/latest/admin/release-schedule/ — release schedule page confirms v15.0 is LTS with EOL July 15, 2027 and v11.0 EOL July 16, 2026, exactly as claimed. (3) lwn.net/Articles/1068001/ — LWN article confirms repository-specific access tokens, UI improvements, and broader Actions changes; notes LTS backing through July 15, 2027. (4) linuxiac.com — confirms OIDC for short-lived tokens replacing long-lived static secrets, Forgejo Runner >12.5 requirement, ephemeral runner single-job execution, web-based registration workflow, cookie change causing re-logins, and rootless Docker config path migration. All claims in the article are directly supported by their attributed sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No inaccuracies detected. All specific claims — release date, 100th release milestone, LTS window dates, repository token scoping behavior, OIDC requirements (Runner >12.5), ephemeral runner credential deletion, breaking changes — are confirmed by the cited sources. The previous review concern about a missing source URL has been resolved; the release schedule URL is now correctly included in sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. The Analysis section offers a measured perspective on the security tradeoffs without overstating the significance of the changes.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Forgejo articles found in src/content/articles/2026-04/. This is new coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong second submission that fully addresses the first-round finding. All four sources are accessible and support the claims attributed to them. The article is factually accurate, well-sourced, and appropriate in scope and tone. Ready for publication.
